diagrams.title: Coprocess Communication Architecture
diagrams.description: Shows the bidirectional pipe between shell and coprocess process, with file descriptor mappings
elements: Pipe: Shell Write End → Coproc stdin
Pipe: Coproc stdout → Shell Read End
Label: NAME[1] for write, NAME[0] for read
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how data flows bidirectionally between shell and coprocess through dedicated file descriptors

diagrams.title: File Descriptor Table During Coproc
diagrams.description: Detailed view of file descriptor table in both parent shell and child coprocess
elements: Arrows showing FD 10 connected to child's stdout
Arrows showing FD 11 connected to child's stdin
Labels showing which FDs are closed in each process
diagrams.purpose: Understand how shell redirects stdin/stdout of child process to pipe ends and what FDs parent retains

diagrams.title: Coprocess Lifecycle States
diagrams.description: State diagram showing transitions from creation through communication to termination
elements: States: Created → Communicating → EOF Sent → Terminated
Transitions: coproc command, read/write operations, close(1), wait
Edge cases: Signal received, Broken pipe, Buffer full
diagrams.purpose: Understand the lifecycle and identify where issues like hangs or blocking can occur
